title: GrowthX <> Assort Health - Partnership Q&A with Marcel

That sounds like an incredible device.
I'll bring Brooke in here and you saw the note about how they were looking at directive initially.
I did not.
Sorry.

All right, cool.
Beautiful.
I'll kind of tee you.
Hey, Brooke.
Happy Friday.
Hey, guys.

Oh, good.
Happy Friday.
You're in the Bay Area?
Yes.
Yep.

Oh, nice.
Nice.

Oh, that'd be awesome.

It's in South Park, like across the street from Blue Bottle.
Oh, my gosh.
That's so funny.
I just started at Assort this month, but my old office was right there.
Oh, no way.
So we're, you know, Toyota Ventures right there?
Yeah.
So we're the entire second floor.
It on, like, Second and Townsend.

But we're, we are across the street from the Transamerica building.
So it's, like, a 12-ish minute walk in the other direction from, like, Montgomery.
So we're, we're nearby, Tyler, unlike Houston, where you just drive.
I grew up in Houston, so I can, I can make fun of Houston, you know.
Yeah.
You just drive and drive and drive.
that's how I, I grew up In Francisco, it's like, depending where you are, it can be different between a quick five-minute thing and a 30-minute thing.
Yeah, yeah.
Even though it's like.
No, I know, I grew up You're like, should I just walk at this point?
I grew up in, you know, in Dallas, so.
Oh, you did?
No way.
Yeah, and I had, I had my, I had my Muni commute.
You know, I lived three years in the city.
Had my Muni commute to the financial district.
That's right.
I keep forgetting that.
don't know why.
A lot of good, a lot of good dogs in Dubose Park.
You know, I know my places.
Oh.
Oh.
That's right.
That's right.
Why did I forget that, Tyler?
I don't know.
Okay.
But Brooke, so nice to meet you.
likewise.
I'm super excited about you guys.
And honestly, I think I, like I said, started as VP of Marketing a few weeks ago.
So there's a few kind of like fractional team members and contractors, but, you know, Assort has been growing, you know, like pretty insanely, almost like in spite of marketing.
Like we haven't really done much.
And, you know, I really believe in the value of like organic search.
And, you know, the power of, like, organic motions and kind of, like, investing in the foundation.
And so, but I will say, I think Assort should have hired me or a version of me, like, at least six months or a year ago.
Like, feel like I'm kind of in a position where I'm having to, like, really make the case.

I mean, we're going to invest in SEO, GEO, but, like, you know, my first, like, couple weeks, I'm, like, thrown into meetings about how we're behind on pipeline targets, hit plan, you know, and, like, what are we going to do?
And what's your biggest competitor right now?
Is it doing nothing?
Or how do you think about it?
Um, it's, I, that we do have.
We competitors.
I think we have like kind of two flavors and then there's doing nothing, but like the two flavors.
to providers only, right?
We sell to provider groups.
We just closed our first health system yesterday, which is really exciting.
And I think we just hired a VP of health system sales and there's like a small kind of like tiger team focused on like expanding into the health system market.
But our bread and butter is like specialty practices like ortho, OBGYN, DERM.
There are some kind of like general practices also like smaller hospitals or like federally qualified health centers, community health centers.
And on the hospital side, we're selling pretty much to like the outpatient and like ambulatory side.
So like, you know, stuff that you would schedule ahead.
But.
But, yeah, so there's a couple, like, we were a first mover in the market, and there's a couple other entrants that have popped up that are, in a lot of cases, like, lower quality and pricing lower.
So those, there's one called Transform9, Elise AI, which, that one's a weird one.
I don't know if you guys are familiar.
They, like, are a unicorn in, like, property management, like, voice AI for property management.
And then they also have, like, if you go to their website, the, like, welcome page is, like, are you here for property management or are you here for healthcare?
But they do have a growing, like, healthcare presence.
We don't see them a ton, but, like, they technically are a competitor.
And then, and then there's another one called Hiro, which is the one that we see most in our health system sales.
So.
So.
Those are like the kind of similar, like more AI native, like competitors.

And then we have this other flavor of like legacy patient engagement software that's been around forever that is now kind of like adding on voice AI capabilities, which that's kind of an interesting one.

Because what then we're doing as a result is actually like our bread and butter is like answering inbound calls from patients via voice AI, helping them, you know, avoid long wait times, getting them scheduled accurately.
And we just have like a ton of accuracy and honestly can be like very precise, even in some cases more accurate and precise than like a new like class.
Are you guys using DeepGram, do you know?
Um, I am sure we are.
I've heard it a lot.
I think, I think.
I I don't know.
I'm assuming you know my background a little bit.
Yeah, I'm pretty sure.
I mean, what I do know is our, my, I reported to, there was actually, we have like a co-CEO model, but I reported to like the non-technical founder who runs GoToMarket.
And he was, he like, I don't, I don't actually know how he knew of you guys, but he said he's heard good things and I should look into you.
So, um, it's probably through DeepGroom because like a lot, that's kind of how we, um, but, uh, but anyways, like that, that makes sense.
And what, just really quickly, um, just out of curiosity.
So like what I'm hearing is there's a ton of pressure on Pipeline.
You're coming in, they're like, Brooke, you're the silver bullet, right?
And we, we got to supercharge you, uh, in some ways I'm, kind of half joking.
Yeah, no, I mean, I think, yeah, what I would say is this all, this actually all came up in like the last couple.
A of days where initially, you know, I'm, I've been, I would say, allocating my time, a lot of time to hiring a team.
Like I think in a perfect world, I'd be spending like half of my time on that because there's so many needs.
And then I do have a product marketing contractor who's pretty, pretty solid.
I have an events and field marketing person.
And, you know, and then like a lot of the foundational demand gen work just like, you know, isn't, it has not been done.
And also like I've shared with Tyler, our site conversion just isn't that great right now.
And there's like another work stream.
We're working with a product marketing kind of like consultancy to help us revamp our homepage and hopefully reduce bounce rate and just get like more like recognition coming to our site.
But, um, but essentially.
Essentially, earlier this week, my CEO asked me, can we spend 50% of your time on IC demand gen work and then 30% on hiring and 20% on firefighting?
So I'm kind of coming back to him with like, look, like there are some quick wins.
We can, we can, you know, put some paid campaigns together that are like not really relying on our website to do the heavy lifting.
I'm thinking like LinkedIn conversation ads or like LinkedIn, like video direct response ads, book a demo type things.
But like, essentially, if we're talking like I'm going to spend 50% of my time on demand gen, and I'm also recommending that I don't do that.

But, but I need like, I need you to understand that, that a big chunk of that is going to be working on increasing our organic traffic and increasing our site conversion, because that's what's going to help us.
Actually scale pipelines sustainably.
And like the pipeline targets are only growing.
So like, don't want to be short-sighted to say like, oh, we need, you know, we need to pour a bunch of money into paid in two months to fill this gap because that's just going to extend.
Yeah.
That makes sense.
And like, as you're talking, like, it really, it really made me think of one of our customers, Tavern.
It was funny because I shared those on the Scudos channel this week.
A WhatsApp between me and the founder.
It wasn't anything like, but he was essentially like, oh my God, like we have so much traffic, we don't know what to do with it.
And I'm not just like saying this because I was kind of like, what, what is happening right now?
But just like three months ago, he texted me, not frustrated, just like, man, like I'm under a lot of pressure.
so hard to find a marketing leader.
And then now he's like, found a marketing leader.
And he's like, oh, I'm so glad I started six months ago with you guys.
Um, but, but I think like there are, you know,
So there are ways to kind of think about like, like accelerating, you know, and, and this is the tavern who I was talking about, right?
Like, and I think what's really cool about the space that you're in is this is like, there's a lot of angles, but there's also a lot of like low intent angles, right?
Like there's not an obscene amount, like that, that's the challenge of voice AI more than anything.
Like there's not a lot of like, oh, I am searching for a voice AI solution and I'm an old school company or something like that, right?
So, so then like the, the, the, the tactic has to be like slightly different.
Like for example, I don't know if Tyler, you should, you should Brooke, but with Bappy, like we had these use case pages that were like programmatic, right?

Like, so we can start with the tutorial because I'll get you like the quick wins, but there are a lot of things around like extending into use cases or different angles or for tavern, for example, like we started.
And this is like super high intent traffic.
It turns out we started looking at like things.

And again, you can still do this through editorial, but explaining like malpractices, explaining like regulations, you know, like, and some of this stuff is doing like really, really well, you know, but I think with school with us, like in that eight weeks, first eight weeks, like we have literally like, it's like an extension of sales, right?
So we're really super charging to try and accelerate, to try to get you like really strong signals.
And so even though you have like a product marketing contractor, like hopefully we can do a ton of audits for you.
We will set your entire like map, all the buyer questions that are out there in the market, understand your personas better, give you entire like content strategy, right?
And start to execute really quickly.
And then if there's any blockers on your site, we can't, we have for the.

Deploy engineers and designers if we need to, you know, like, let's say, like, your site map is broken or there's some technical issue or, hey, there's some really big issue with how your blog pages are displayed or something like that.
Like, we were accelerants.
We jump in there.
And so usually, like, we have a couple kicking off right now.

I was just talking to the new team of CoreLogix, same thing, like, just starting, you know, and it's like, I think it's a good way for us to just, try to accelerate as much as possible, you know, because I've been in your shoes where when I joined Scale AI, it was me and, like, two people and we're, like, 100 million.
And the CEO is like, you got to get to 300 million.
By the way, you have no budget.
In that case, it's like, you know, so you're just like, I just need help to go as fast as possible, hopefully, you know.
Yeah.
Yeah.
I mean, I think, I mean, I think it would be great.
It's not going to be a requirement.
To sign the contract.

But I do think I need to think through how I want to get my CEO kind of like at least educated and kind of like bought in on like the value of me spending a lot of my time, you know, especially in that eight-week sprint with you guys, making sure you're set up for success.
Which I think, you know, he will, he will definitely get it.
But yeah, I think he's, he's, he's like an ideas guy and has like a lot of things.
I'm same way.
Connect me, connect me.
I'm happy to have a board meeting on Tuesday, but I don't want to delay anything.
Because like, yeah, no, I think, I mean, I think I can risk, like, because there's, you know, like, that eight-week period.
And, and I think like, the, the main thing is just getting his schedule so that.
Yeah.
Like, you know, you're not delaying like six weeks out.
But I'll say one thing because this does come up a lot.
We, I'd say about half of our customers, we work directly with founder or one single person marketing team or very lean marketing team.
do have public companies and we do have like skill ups, but like even Brex, for example, just got acquired by Capital One.
But it's like we have one person that handles the whole thing on their end, right?
Like, and so the goal, it's more about like, it's more about who are the experts on your end that can help us define what quality looks like.
Because quality is not just like a one thing.
It's like layers, right?
Like, what are the sources of research that are reputable?
What are the, you know, the regulations you're under?
What are the things you care about?
Like, who are the language you want to come across?
Like, sometimes someone really cares about this technical part and then somebody else cares really about sounding a certain way, right?
Like, so it's defining that.
And once we have a definition, then, then it's a lot.
Faster, but usually the burden is not on you.
It's on you to just tell us who to talk to.
And we go do the hour interviews in the first couple of weeks.
We go do all of that.
But it is on you to tell us if we're heading in the right direction.
And I think where things don't go as well is when you're like week four or five and the main stakeholder got busy, didn't pull in a right person that has strong opinions.
And then the person comes on week five.
And as far as we're concerned, it looks great.
And they come in like, no, I don't like this, this, this, and this on how the output is looking like.
And then that's when it's like, then we're kind of under the gun, right?
It's like, oh, okay, now we got to reprove ourselves, you know?
So the earlier we do it, then the smoother it is.
And the last time, hopefully, it will consume from you, you know?
Yep, totally.
Because you get a team of six people like forward deploy to you, like immediately, essentially, you know?
And there are people that.
I pulled together personally, like, cherry-picked myself.
Awesome.
Awesome.
Yeah, I'm super excited.
I added a couple, I don't know if you saw, Tyler, like, a couple just, like, comments in the contract.
Do you see those?
Oh, you're on mute.
I didn't get an email notification, but I can check it out, like, right now.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, mean, the main thing was just, I guess, I have two, like, just philosophical questions.

I'm assuming, okay, so the first one is, I'm assuming you guys are pretty, and I understand why, like, adamant about, like, the 12-month contract, um, which I think makes sense, but just wanted to hear, like, if that's an area.
So of flexibility or like, you know, having an opt-out clause after six months, but like having, I mean, I think I know that this investment does take time.
And like, that's that founder that you were talking to, Marcel, it's like, you know, you have to give it, you have to be patient and give it time.
But, but yeah, just talk, maybe talk to me about that.
Yeah.
And if you ever are flexible.
The way to think about it is like the simplest way to explain to people is that we are VC funded tech company.
And we have a team of 20 engineers and we burn money, right?
99.9999% of agencies, you know, that, that deliver something like an agency in this space or any space for that matter has to run a highly profitable business.
Right.
And so you're almost like gaining the benefit of like the fact that we have a hundred people.
And our ratio of like, we have 60 of those 100 people in our company that are like forward deployed.
These people are, you know, people that are working with these top brands, like Lovable, Ramp, Webflow, Brax, like Sentinel One, Abnormal.
And they're the caliber of people that it will cost you way, way, way more just if you even just hire them for their time that they're going to spend on you, right?
And so in a lot of ways, like there's that, but then there's also like our platform, which we're only, you know, a month or so away from opening up to customers.

And that's how, think of it as like we built an entire platform that our team uses and now and reports from and interact with you and does all the work for you because most of our teams don't want to go in there and edit things and do the work.
And, you know, but we are literally a month or so away from giving you access to that.
So part of us would be included, right?
And so with that, though, if we do monthly...
So quarterly, from our experience, what is essential?
Yeah, I mean, I would not ever propose that.
I think it's more like, yeah, the only thing I would ever propose is like a six-month kind of like opt-out clause or something if we're six months in and it's just not working.
But I do understand.
And I think that's probably less important.
And I mean, the other thing that I, the other, I don't know if you want to talk at all about this, Tyler, the weekly things.
Yeah, I was just going to say like, absolutely, right?
Like, let's set up weekly syncs with your team.
Honestly, we can always like push it to bi-weekly, but I'd rather the weekly be like back-to-back season.
Yeah.
I think that's totally fair.
How do you feel, Marcel?
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.
And just so you understand, so like, to your earlier point, so in the first...
Eight weeks.
Like, we are more than weekly, right?
Like, we're in there.

Like, the comments, and I'm happy to connect you with other customers, like, are usually, like, this is the most output I've seen from any vendor I've ever worked with in the shortest amount of time I've ever seen.

Like, so I don't want to oversell, but it's truly, like, we, what I'm doing is I'm taking, like, six months of what I used to do as a team, recruiting a team, all the kind of, like, product marketing, context generation, everything, and trying to squeeze it into the first few weeks and then start to calibrate and push as hard as possible.
But also, like, if the concern is with your CEO or on anything on the long term, that's why we have the eight weeks, right?
And we have about an 80% conversion from what we call strategy spread to growth execution, right?
And so it just tells you that, like, we have hired customers before, we've been hired before, we're not perfect.
And we're not for everyone.

And you get in when you, you know, you get out what you put in, but I am extremely confident in the talent we have and the tech we have and that we're learning with like, like we get so many learnings by working with all these amazing brands, you know?
Yeah.

But, but it's also like, if people, there's so many times when the six month mark, if you like try to measure the perfect ROI on the six month mark, you would have so much leverage to say, you know, I need you to cut your costs by 50%.
And that has happened before.
And so after that, we're like, you know, it's not worth it.
One, two, we wouldn't be able to be a sustainable VC funded model if we didn't have an ARR.
And so then you wouldn't have the benefit we would have to charge.
Just to give you an idea, like some of these agencies that you probably heard of that been on like Lenny's podcast and others, they're like 10x more expensive than we are.
And, and so it's like the reason for that is because they, you know, they have to run a completely different, different.
So that's the way to hopefully help you explain.
Yeah, so I guess.
Yes.
In the interest of time, there's three things that would be super helpful.
The first is I did make a comment just on like totally align on the upfront like investment.
think it was like 40K.
And then I did share, obviously, I had shared a proposal from another, I mean, very different model, but different agency.
So I added a comment around flexibility on like the monthly rate.
So you don't have to answer me now.
But if you could kind of think on that and get back to me on what is possible there, that's the first thing.
The second thing, it would be amazing.
And just like doing my due diligence, you know, especially knowing the situation that I'm in.
If I could talk to like a customer reference, like, but, you know, I'd love to connect you with Megan, who's the new VP of marketing at Tavern.
Yeah, perfect.
There's similar stage as you, similar domain authority as you.
And she just joined about five weeks ago, six weeks ago.
And so she's like, and I've already met with her in person.
You know, we spent time, so she's fully up to speed.
And so she can tell you exactly like what it's been like.
That would be amazing.
Being a lean team and coming in and having that support already.
Yeah, that would be amazing.
The last thing is probably more for you, Tyler.
I know you've sent over like a ton of resources and I really appreciate it.
I think if I could just ask you to like, I have to basically make a case to my CEO on like why growthx, honestly over directive, which I think you've already made some really good points.
But like, honestly, just kind of nothing long, but like a couple paragraphs on like why, why it's why this investment makes sense.
And like just some of the traffic, like I basically.
I kind of set some conservative expectations for 2026 on like increasing our organic site traffic or just site traffic in general, non-paid, I guess, like organic direct and referral.
I set some like pretty conservative targets and then same with like site conversion increase, like pretty conservative.
So I think it was like, you know, 40% increase in referral, like organic traffic, and then something like, you know, 40, 50% increase in site conversion, which technically would help us hit our goal.
Like that, if that happened together, we would hit our like inbound, like pipeline goal.
Um, but I, I basically was like my, my understanding of like GrowthX is like, if we work with them, we're going to blow this traffic goal out of the water.
And then it's really up to us on the conversion side.
But I think as much as you can like.
Like, just explain what you guys do, and I think that would be super helpful, because that's going to be the last thing for me to, like, push the contract through.
I think he, like, he trusts me and, like, is looking to me for a recommendation, but I still want to be, you know.
And also, like, Tyler and I can work on that and send it to you, and then separately, happy to spend time with your co-CEO if helpful, because I've also been in the space.
And I don't know if they saw, but Deepgram just raised a 1.3 billion valuation, you know, really healthy round.
And when I joined, hadn't done a product launch in 18 months, and their traffic was, like, nothing.
And, you know, and so I think I learned a lot from that, and I've been in the space for so long, just in case it's helpful.
I'm sure he knows his  and doesn't need help, but, you know, it's always nice talking to a fellow person, you know, sometimes.
And it's...
it's...

The TLDR, and we'll send you this, it's ultimately like our platform and also the talent, the way we recruit talent, like, you know, and how we vet everybody, you know, and I think, and then outside of that is really like, like, I've taken everything I've done in the last 15 years, like, you know, HashiCorp went public, ServiceSight went public, Scale.ai got bought by, half bought by Meta by 29 billion, you know, DeepPram is doing amazing, I work at IBM, HP, like, we really, every single time, like, the patterns were the same, and that's really like, our playbook and our process is really like, but then every single day as we're working with these other companies, they're also growing a lot and we're helping them, we're constantly, like, reiterating the pattern.
So our platform is not just this, like, bland platform, a lot of it is, like, figuring out the patterns that work, you know, so, but, like, we'll summarize it in nice, like, we should do that anyway.
Yeah, yeah, and
I think, like, honestly, don't spend too much time on it.
Like, think of it as, like, think of it as more or less, like, in my words, explaining, like, why you guys make sense.
So it's really more around, like, this is what I think we're going to be able to get.
This is why I think we're going to be able to get better results from them versus, like, a traditional agency.
And, you know, because of Marcel's background and, like, the track record that they have, you know, so it's really about that.
But I really appreciate it.
And, yeah, I mean, let's, if you want to, I can't remember her name, but the Tavern VP of Marketing.
Yeah, Megan, yeah, Tyler, we can connect offline on that.
And, Brooke, are you around next week?
We're actually hosting a CMO marketing leader dinner on Wednesday, if you're around.
But, no.
So no worries if not, we're constantly posting things.
What time?
Do you know what time it's at?
I have, I do have plans Wednesday, but.
It's at, it's at Birdsong at 5.30.
But if you were like a little late, I'm sure that's fine too.
I have plans at like 7.30.
Do you think it's going to go?
I mean, I would, I mean, would be awesome to at least come and get to meet some, some people.
There's going to be both customers, friends, and you know, like, it's all like, mostly like marketing leaders.
And, um, around, uh, either, either way.
Um, sorry, I missed, like we had a, a breakfast yesterday too, in the office.
No, that's awesome.
I love it.
We're constantly like doing stuff too.
So now that you're local, you can be part of the crew, you know.
Amazing.
All right.
Thank you guys.
I will.
Awesome.
Brooke, thank you so much.
We'll, we'll talk soon.
Okay.
Thanks guys.
Brooke.
Bye.
Great meeting you, Marcel.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Finalize the GrowthX partnership for Assort Health's marketing acceleration.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1440.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Urgent Pipeline Pressure:** Assort Health's CEO is pushing for immediate pipeline growth, creating a conflict with the new VP of Marketing's (Brooke) strategy to build a sustainable organic foundation.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=519.0)
  - [**GrowthX as the Solution:** GrowthX offers a rapid-deployment team and platform to deliver foundational marketing work (audits, strategy) in an 8-week "Strategy Sprint," providing a fast-track to sustainable growth.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=786.0)
  - [**Contract Terms:** The 12-month term is non-negotiable, as it is required to fund GrowthX's VC-backed model. Weekly syncs are approved. A request for flexibility on the monthly rate is pending review.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1105.0)
  - [**CEO Buy-in Required:** Brooke needs a formal justification for her CEO, comparing GrowthX to Directive, before signing. GrowthX will provide a written summary and a customer reference.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1522.0)

## Topics

### Assort Health's Marketing Challenge

  - [Brooke, the new VP of Marketing, faces intense pressure to meet pipeline targets.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=250.0)
  - [**CEO's Mandate:** 50% of Brooke's time on IC demand gen, 30% on hiring, 20% on firefighting.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
  - [**Brooke's Strategy:** Build a sustainable organic foundation (SEO, site conversion) to avoid a short-term, expensive paid ad cycle.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=637.0)
  - [**Competitive Landscape:**](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=291.0)
      - [**AI-native:** Transform9, Elise AI, Hiro](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
      - [**Legacy:** Patient engagement software adding AI features](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=428.0)

### GrowthX's Proposed Solution

  - [**Model:** A VC-backed tech company, not a traditional agency, with a team of 100 (60 forward-deployed) and a proprietary platform.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1166.0)
  - [**8-Week Strategy Sprint:** A rapid-deployment team delivers 6 months of work in 8 weeks, including:](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=786.0)
      - [Audits & persona mapping](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=816.0)
      - [Content strategy](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=816.0)
      - [Technical fixes (via deploy engineers)](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=836.0)
  - [**Goal:** Provide quick wins and a clear roadmap, acting as an extension of the sales team.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=786.0)
  - [**Success Metric:** 80% of clients convert from the Strategy Sprint to ongoing growth execution.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1356.0)

### Contract & Logistics

  - [**Contract Term:** The 12-month term is non-negotiable.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1116.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The term is required to fund GrowthX's VC-backed model and prevent clients from using a 6-month opt-out clause to renegotiate pricing just as ROI begins to materialize.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1395.0)
  - [**Sync Cadence:** Weekly syncs are approved.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1287.0)
  - [**Monthly Rate:** A request for flexibility on the monthly rate is pending review.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1440.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX (Marcel):**](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1440.0)
      - [Connect Brooke with Megan (VP Marketing, Tavern) for a customer reference.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1476.0)
      - [Review the monthly rate flexibility request and provide an answer.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1440.0)
  - [**GrowthX (Tyler):**](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1522.0)
      - [Draft a formal justification for Brooke's CEO, comparing GrowthX to Directive and explaining the value proposition.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1522.0)
  - [**Assort Health (Brooke):**](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1522.0)
      - [Use the provided materials to secure CEO buy-in for the partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/ioeTCarBychTjsFMMNyzTyeCeHqBaQwA?tab=summary&timestamp=1522.0)
